projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The gradual decrease in connected mobile devices, dropping to 8 at 06:53 AM, indicates the family is waking up and beginning to leave the house for Saturday activities. Recognizing this departure pattern allows the system to proactively adjust security and energy settings for an 'away' state.

The sustained 8-9 device presence combined with zero camera motion over four hours confirms the family is gathered in a single common area for a prolonged sedentary activity, explaining the lack of cross-room movement. This is a normal Friday evening pattern.

The sustained 8-device presence with zero camera motion confirms the family is settled in a single, camera-blind zone for a typical Friday evening gathering, and the temporary 9-device spike around 19:07–19:20 likely reflects a brief guest or device connection rather than an anomaly.

The consistent 8-device presence paired with zero camera motion over four hours suggests the household is either engaged in a prolonged sedentary activity or the cameras may be offline/obstructed; a quick system check is recommended to rule out a hardware issue.

The stable 8-device presence combined with zero camera motion over the past four hours suggests the cameras may be misconfigured or pointed away from active zones, as a Friday afternoon with two kids and two energetic dogs typically generates movement that should trigger alerts.

The stable 7–9 device count combined with zero camera motion on a Friday afternoon confirms the school-age children are at school with their devices persisting on the network, while the adults are likely home but stationary in camera-blind zones; this resolves the presence discrepancy as a normal routine pattern rather than an anomaly.

The sustained 7–9 device count with zero camera motion over four hours indicates devices are likely stationary (charging or left unattended) rather than actively in use, suggesting a presence sensor glitch or a minor security/privacy concern worth verifying.

The persistent 7–9 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours on a Friday afternoon strongly indicates Wi-Fi presence detection is being skewed by background IoT device scanning rather than actual human activity, which may cause unnecessary automation triggers for an unoccupied or still home.

The sustained 7–10 device count paired with zero camera motion over four hours indicates the extra connections are stationary smart home gadgets and personal electronics rather than additional guests, confirming the low-severity network correlation and reflecting normal weekend device usage.

The home consistently reports 9–10 connected mobile devices, which is unusually high for a four-person household and, combined with zero camera motion events, strongly suggests a network configuration issue misclassifying IoT devices as mobile or a previously connected device lingering. This warrants a quick network check to rule out unauthorized access or configuration drift.
